title: Call of Duty: World at War

notes: Runs great out the box. Performance is un-optimised; playing on lowest settings and highest settings make no discernible difference to FPS. Maintains 25+ FPS going right up to 60 FPS, all over the place. Doesn't ruin the experience for me.
title: Call of Duty: World at War

notes: Launches and loads zombies and can be played normally. Froze after attempting to change texture settings resolution can be changed without issues minor stutters present.
title: Call of Duty: World at War

gpu: X.Org AMD Radeon (TM) R9 Fury Series (FIJI, DRM 3.26.0, 4.18.9-arch1-1-ARCH, LLVM 6.0.1)
notes: The first mission in the campaign runs at 25-35 fps with lots of structures around, and has reached a maximum of 91 fps when in wooded areas or areas with little to render. All settings can be changed including resolution. Mouse seems to jump around or stutter, turning on mouse smoothing in the options menu of the game seems to help a lot. In zombies, same fps experiences except after a while of playing and opening doors to the rest of the map, the lag seems to balance out and I eventually got 60 fps stable in areas that were previously laggy. I have not tested multiplayer.
title: Call of Duty: World at War

title: Call of Duty: World at War

notes: I had to use winetricks to manually install DirectX 9 to make sound work. I set my Proton prefix to use Windows Vista as the WINEVER. I set the config to use an emulated desktop, and allowed it to capture the mouse. I also disabled the use of GLSL shadering, and manually specified my amount of VRAM. Next, I noticed WaW was slow. I believe it's tied to the game's config file incorrectly setting processor speed and available system RAM. The lines that read "seta sys_configureGHz" and "seta sys_sysMB" were way below my system specs. I set my processor speed to what Advanced Warfare had, in its file, 19.2. I don't know if 19.2 will work for everyone, but it should work on R5 1600s. If you have Advanced Warfare or a CoD game from about 2012-2014, the GHz value given by that should work for WaW. Even though I have 16 gigs of ram to use, I set mine to 4096. 4GB is more than enough for a 2008 game. After that, I'm able to run the game perfectly.

title: Call of Duty: World at War

notes: Works out of the box, performance is not the same as on windows. Some stutter from time to time. Handy commands ingame (you will need to enable console in options) /cg_drawfps 1 (show fps counter) /com_maxfps 250 (set fps cap) /cg_fov 80 (set fov)

notes: Now that FAudio is integrated into Proton, the audio sounds perfect, with no stutters. Could even qualify for the whitelist.
